key_issues: N1 CRITICAL (new gap from the revision): the warnings accessor has no defined, port-compatible home. Item 010's compiled-validator is a frozen single-field struct (validate-proc) that item 011 is forbidden to redefine, and provider-compile's generic returns a bare compiled-validator? — so a `compiled-validator-warnings` accessor is unimplementable and provider-compile cannot 'also return' warnings without widening the port. The spec lists three incompatible options (line 154) without choosing; at least one is impossible. Worse, the simplest reading (one mutable warnings field on the provider) is directly contradicted by the S7 'two handles from one provider' test, which compiles twice and would overwrite the slot. Pin ONE mechanism keyed per-compile and add a fixture: compile h1(minLength) then h2(pattern) from the same provider, assert h1 warnings name minLength and h2 name pattern (not overwritten).

N2 CRITICAL: warn-once cardinality is asserted only on the stderr channel; the recorded-list channel's growth across validates is offered as an 'equivalently' alternative, not a conjunction. A provider could append to the warnings list every validate while eprintf-ing once. Require BOTH: stderr once at compile AND recorded-list length identical after 0 and 3 validates.

N3 CRITICAL: the warn-once test captures via (parameterize ([current-error-port (open-output-string)]) …), but if emission uses log-warning (offered as an option, line 58) it writes to the logging system NOT current-error-port, so the capture sees nothing and 'exactly one warning' passes vacuously (zero is not >1). Pin emission to eprintf/current-error-port so the capture is valid, or require a make-log-receiver count; cleanest is to make the recorded-list count the load-bearing warn-once check.

RESOLVED (audit): iter-001 C1 collect-all (exact (= 2 (length …)) counts), C2/C3 non-object/non-array clean rejection (check-not-exn on every crash-class value + no-`type` variants), C4 deferred ignore-with-warning uniform + warn-once, C5 format-on-non-string/unknown-format, C6 adversarial rejects + documented limitation per format, and all of S1–S8 are genuinely pinned with ACs and fixtures — not merely named. The keyword/value/wrong-type surface is airtight.
SUGGESTED S-a: C1×C4 interaction untested — {type:number,minimum:0} on "x" must yield exactly 1 error (type), not 2 (ignored minimum must contribute 0, no phantom 'skipped' pseudo-error).

SUGGESTED S-b/S-e/S-f: unknown-format-records-even-when-type-fails ({type:string,format:ipv4} on 42 → 1 error + ipv4 still recorded); enum nested in properties must carry its path segment ('("color")); empty {properties:{}} hash must not crash (hash-ref-assuming-present).

SUGGESTED S-c/S-d: malformed deferred-keyword VALUE ((hasheq 'minLength "three")) is left 'pick whichever' but the chosen branch must be required to have a test; and whether check-schema-shape recurses into sub-schemas (malformed nested {type:"stringg"}) is unpinned — recommend recurse + fail-fast at compile, else a malformed deep sub-schema hides until a value reaches it.
